calendar.matters:
CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Sep-29-2017 Off Calendar

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Aug-18-2017 Continued To Oct-18-17 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Aug-23-2017 Sustained
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Larry White, G-37720, P.O. Box 5001, Calipatria, CA 92233; Plaintiff/Opposing party in Pro Per appearing telephonically on Speaker on the Court's Phone.
Kelly Collins, Deputy City Attorney (415-554-3914)) for Defendant/Moving party CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO

RULING - A R G U E D. The Court announces the tentative ruling on the record for the Plaintiff. (as fully reported in the reporter's notes) The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO DEMURRER TO PLAINTIFF'S FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT.

Defendant City and County of San Francisco's demurrer to the first amended complaint is sustained without leave to amend. The court grants the City's request for judicial notice. Assuming, without deciding, that plaintiff Larry White's complaint was timely filed, Mr. White's amended complaint fails to state a claim for a number of reasons. First, Mr. White has not provided a statutory basis for the City's liability showing that the city is not covered by the immunity provided in Government Code 815. Second, judicial officers are considered employees of the State of California and Mr. White has provided no allegations that would support holding the City liable for the actions of Judge Alvarado and Judge Yaggy. Finally, judicially noticeable documents establish that Judge Alvarado and Judge Yaggy had taken the oath of officer before doing the acts that form the basis of Mr. White's complaint. (Request for Judicial Notice, Exhibits A & B.) Mr. White cites the requirement contained in Government Code 1363(3) which requires that oaths of office be filed with the Secretary of State's office. Mr. White provides no evidence that failure to provide a copy of a judicial oath of office restricts a judge from carrying out his or her judicial duties. Mr. White relies on Legerton v. Chambers (1917) 32 Cal.App. 601, 604, but Legerton deals with appointments made during the period between a gubernatorial election and the certification of election results. Because the City cannot be held liable for the acts of judicial officers and because judicially noticeable documents establish Judge Alvarado and Judge Yaggy had taken the oath of office before performing the acts of which Mr. White complains, Mr. White's amended complaint is barred as a matter of law and leave to amend is therefore denied. (as fully reported in the reporter's notes) Order is signed after the hearing. Judge: Charlene P. Kiesselbach, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Gordon Aiavao, CSR #11216, gaiavao@sftc.org, Reported. =(302/CPK)
